data.title: Private Companies Are Reshaping These 5 Industries: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley Research highlighted five key areas in which private companies are "driving transformative change."

Smaller startups and niche units within larger corporations are "pushing the boundaries of technology and redefining business models and investors have taken notice,” the research report stated. 

Orbital Data Centers Are Seeing Popularity

An increasing number of private companies are developing infrastructure to operate data centers in space. 

"Space-based data centers face challenges such as data governance, regulation and maintenance," Adam Jonas, Morgan Stanley's Embodied AI/Robotics Strategist stated in the report. "But at scale, they offer unique advantages over terrestrial facilities."  

Private companies are working to make orbital data centers achievable. Currently, three companies involved in the projects have raised more than $700 million in funding.

Autonomous Vehicles: The Next Phase

Self-driving cars (autonomous vehicles) are another area in which private companies and investors are seeing an opportunity. 

Morgan Stanley estimates that AV miles driven in the U.S. reached 116 million in 2025 and are projected to grow at an annual rate of 103% through 2032, reaching 16 billion miles.

"Both public and private players in the AV ecosystem—which includes makers of cars, sensors and cameras; software developers; and rideshare companies—are scaling production and forging partnerships to capture this opportunity," said Brian Nowak, head of U.S. Internet Research at Morgan Stanley.

Private Companies Lead In The Operational Technology Space 

The operational technology (OT) security market is led mostly by private companies as the increasing threat of ransomware attacks on manufacturing and infrastructure has highlighted a need for more security.

Large public cybersecurity companies have announced products or acquisitions in the space; private companies will be "best equipped to capitalize on this market opportunity," noted Meta Marshall, Morgan Stanley's cybersecurity and network and equipment analyst.

Artificial Intelligence Uses In The Healthcare Sector

Artificial intelligence, particularly in the healthcare space, is becoming increasingly popular. AI-powered scribes that record patient-provider conversations and notes for electronic health records are taking the strain off of healthcare providers and investors have taken notice.

"AI-enabled startups have raised $3.4 billion over the past five years, accounting for 10 percent of total digital health funding," the report stated. The long-term market opportunity is around $11 billion, according to Morgan Stanley Research. 

The market for AI-scribes is "fragmented," and consolidation will need to occur, the report stated. Companies are considering partnerships and M&A to gain scale.

Drone Manufacturing in the U.S.

Last year, private companies involved in drone technology and manufacturing raised more than $10 billion from global venture capital, up from $4.5 billion in 2024.

The Russia-Ukraine conflict highlighted the need for drones in modern warfare. The industry is mainly produced in China (70 percent). 

Last July, the Department of Defense announced that they aimed to "bolster the domestic manufacturing base," which includes the purchase of millions of drones in the coming years.

The drone market is currently one of the most under-appreciated strata within the embodied AI ecosystem, with ripe opportunity for a number of private players to capture a piece of what could potentially be far larger than the global auto market," said Jonas.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Humana Analysts Slash Their Forecasts After Q4 Results
data.description:
Humana Inc. (NYSE:HUM) posted better-than-expected results for the fourth quarter but issued weak fiscal 2026 outlook on Wednesday.

The U.S. insurer reported its fourth-quarter adjusted loss of $3.96, wider than the loss of $2.16 reported last year, exceeding the consensus loss estimate of $4.00. The company reported sales of $32.64 billion, up from $29.19 billion a year ago, beating the consensus of $32.08 billion.

Humana expects fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings per share of at least $9, compared to the Wall Street estimate of $11.92.

"We were pleased with our solid financial performance and operational progress in 2025," said Humana President and CEO Jim Rechtin.

Humana shares rose 2.5% to trade at $179.80 on Thursday.

These analysts made changes to their price targets on Humana following earnings announcement.

  • Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Sarah James maintained Humana with a Neutral and lowered the price target from $290 to $201.
  • Guggenheim analyst Jason Cassorla maintained the stock with a Buy and lowered the price target from $312 to $252.
  • RBC Capital analyst Ben Hendrix downgraded Humana from Outperform to Sector Perform and cut the price target from $322 to $189.

Considering buying HUM stock? Here’s what analysts think:

data.title: 10 Consumer Discretionary Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
data.description:
This whale alert can help traders discover the next big trading opportunities.

Whales are entities with large sums of money and we track their transactions here at Benzinga on our options activity scanner.

Traders will search for circumstances when the market estimation of an option diverges heavily from its normal worth. High amounts of trading activity could push option prices to exaggerated or underestimated levels.

Below are some instances of options activity happening in the Consumer Discretionary sector:

Explanation

These itemized elaborations have been created using the accompanying table.

• For TSLA (NASDAQ:TSLA), we notice a put option sweep that happens to be bearish, expiring in 1 day(s) on February 13, 2026. This event was a transfer of 200 contract(s) at a $415.00 strike. This particular put needed to be split into 19 different trades to become filled. The total cost received by the writing party (or parties) was $72.9K, with a price of $364.0 per contract. There were 6306 open contracts at this strike prior to today, and today 24814 contract(s) were bought and sold.

Options Alert Terminology
- Call Contracts: The right to buy shares as indicated in the contract.
- Put Contracts: The right to sell shares as indicated in the contract.
- Expiration Date: When the contract expires. One must act on the contract by this date if one wants to use it.
- Premium/Option Price: The price of the contract.

For more information, visit our Guide to Understanding Options Alerts or read more about unusual options activity.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.

data.title: Quince Therapeutics Stock Plunges As Filing Shows Reverse Merger Plans, Bankruptcy Risks
data.description:
Quince Therapeutics Inc (NASDAQ:QNCX) stock is trading sharply lower Thursday after the biotech company followed up Wednesday's rally with a Form 8-K that underscores how precarious its situation is. Here’s what investors need to know amid this week’s volatility.

  • Quince Therapeutics stock is showing notable weakness. Why are QNCX shares down?

QNCX Stock Tumbles After Strategic Review Euphoria

The filing expands on this week's announcement that Quince hired LifeSci Capital as exclusive financial advisor to explore strategic alternatives, including a potential reverse merger.

The new disclosure shows Quince ended 2025 with about $5.8 million in cash and cash equivalents, $11.9 million in short-term investments and $16.4 million outstanding on an unsecured loan from the European Investment Bank.

Management also confirmed that lead candidate eDSP failed to meet primary or secondary endpoints in the NEAT trial, leaving the company with "no meaningful operations" and no resources to advance programs.

Reverse Merger, Delisting And Bankruptcy Risks Pressure Shares

Quince said the only path to shareholder value is executing a reverse merger with a private company, but noted it has no agreements in place and may not secure a deal on attractive terms, or at all. To fund the process, the company may issue more equity or debt and could use proceeds to pay down obligations.

The 8-K highlights risks around potential Nasdaq delisting, acceleration of the EIB loan and the possibility of Chapter 7 or 11 bankruptcy proceedings, where common shareholders would likely recover little or nothing.

QNCX Shares Plunge Thursday

QNCX Price Action: Quince Therapeutics shares were down 52.07% at 29 cents at the time of publication on Thursday. The stock is approaching its 52-week low of 12 cents, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
